summary: Spain's posture is carried by INCIBE and its INCIBE-CERT, which address the quantum threat and the need to adopt post-quantum cryptography, with the national coordination centre presenting the European public-administration transition roadmap. Parliamentary guidance states that the transition must begin from a complete inventory of cryptographic systems, protocols and algorithms.
govActors:
INCIBE | Spanish National Cybersecurity Institute, leads national cybersecurity work and hosts the national coordination centre (NCC-ES)
INCIBE-CERT | national CERT, publishes guidance and advisories on the quantum threat and post-quantum cryptography
Congreso de los Diputados | Spanish Congress, parliamentary bulletins framing the transition around a complete cryptographic inventory
Oficina Nacional de Prospectiva | national foresight office, trend analysis on post-quantum cryptography
Banco de España | central bank, studies post-quantum cryptography against the store-now-decrypt-later threat
provenance: INCIBE / INCIBE-CERT lead and quantum-threat framing: INCIBE-CERT guidance and advisory (ingested)
EU public-administration transition roadmap presented via NCC-ES: INCIBE (NCC-ES) dossier (ingested)
Transition must start from a complete cryptographic inventory: BOCG bulletin no. 223, Congreso de los Diputados (ingested) - source is a portal redirect link, REVIEW
Store-now-decrypt-later study: Banco de Espana occasional paper and FSR box (ingested) - bde.es flagged, REVIEW
dominantProcess omitted: no standards camp (NIST/ETSI/ISO) named in captured summaries
